Damian Hinds
MP for East Hampshire
In Parliament

Parliamentary activity

  • Tweet

On this page, you can see Damian's latest activity in Parliament, including speeches and written questions to Ministerial departments. 

Oral or Written Questions

Once a month Ministers from each government department answer questions from MPs at the Dispatch Box. There is a limit to the number of questions that can be asked. MPs can also table a written question to the relevant Government department, which are published in Hansard. http://www.parliament.uk/business/publications/hansard/

Adjournment Debate

MPs may be able to raise a constituent's issue in a half-hour Adjournment Debate. To get an adjournment debate, MPs must be successful in a ballot of Members of Parliament or have the subject chosen by the Speaker. The debates are usually the last business of the day and a government minister responds at the end of the debate.

Petitions

Members of Parliament can present a petition to Parliament on behalf of their constituents. The format and wording of the petition need to be in a particular way. For more information or guidance, please visit: Petitions - UK Government and Parliament

Damian's activity in parliament

Damian is currently a member of the Select Committee on Culture, Media and Sport, is co-chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group on Down Syndrome and is a member of a number of All Party Parliamentary Groups including heritage railways, rural services, children's online safety, hospitality and tourism, haemophilia and contaminated blood, AI and youth employment.  He also recently sat on Bill Committees for the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Bill and the IfATE Bill (now Act).

 

This month, Damian has asked the following written questions (click the links below to see the answers from the Government):

Written Answer - Department for Health and Social Care (25 June 2025)
Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of awarding Dental Schools status to the University of Portsmouth Dental Academy.

Written Answers - HM Treasury (23 June 2025)

Damian Hinds: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of merging the Valuation Office Agency and HMRC when dealing with businesses, in the context of changes to Agricultural Property Relief.

Written Answers - Home Office (4 June 2025)

Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent discussions she has had with Police and Crime Commissioners on tackling vehicle nuisance in residential areas.

Written Answers - Home Office (4 June 2025)

Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to give powers to the police to tackle illegally modified exhausts for (a) cars and (b) motorcycles.

Written Answers - Department for Transport (4 June 2025)

Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 8 May to Question HL6929 on Roads: Cameras, if she will issue guidance to (a) police and (b) local authorities on how roadside noise cameras may be used to tackle noise-related anti-social behaviour.

Written Answers - Department for Education (3 June 2025)

Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how much (a) set up, (b) other one-off, (c) food, (d) staffing and (e) other operating cost funding has been allocated to schools for school breakfast pilots in total; and what estimate she has made of the (i) number of eligible children and (ii) average number of breakfasts to be taken per eligible child over the period.

Written Answers - Department for Health and Social Care (3 June 2025)

Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Written Statement of 22 May 2025 on Childhood Obesity, HCWS652, whether the output of TV channels not viewed as live broadcast TV will count as (a) TV and (b) online.

For a full list of the questions Damian has asked in Parliament recently, click here.

News

Westminster Hall debate on government support for businesses in rural areas

Wednesday, 18 June, 2025
Damian spoke in the Westminster Hall debate on rural businesses.

Housing formula will double East Hampshire’s target but still not deliver homes local people can afford

Wednesday, 14 May, 2025
Damian released the following media statement today: East Hampshire MP Damian Hinds has again urged the government to rethink its method for calculating housing need.  Speaking in a Westminster Hall debate on the government’s ‘standard method’, Damian criticised the approach for impos

Greater awareness is needed about the digital landline switch over

Wednesday, 14 May, 2025
During the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) oral questions session today, Damian urged the Minister to take steps to raise awareness of the digital landline switch over, and, specifically, the implications for those currently dependent on their landlines.

Safer Phones Private Members Bill - Second reading

Friday, 7 March, 2025
Damian spoke during today's second reading debate on the Safer Phone's Bill.   Speaking after the debate, Damian said: "The Safer Phones Bill is a missed opportunity, in my view, to push for real reform in this crucial area.

E-petition debate on VAT on independent school fees

Monday, 3 March, 2025
Damian spoke at the e-petition debate on the imposition of VAT on independent school fees and the impact of business rates relief on these schools. Speaking at the time, Damian said: "Over 600 East Hampshire residents signed the petition to Parliament against the imposition of VAT on education.

Damian appointed to shadow education brief

Tuesday, 9 July, 2024
Damian  was last night appointed as shadow education secretary.   Damian said: "Education has always been my first passion and one of the best things about being MP for East Hampshire is working with our excellent local schools, early years settings and colleges.

Community Payback in action

Monday, 25 September, 2023
It was good to visit Hayling Island last week to see offenders clearing the beaches as pay back for their crimes.   Hayling Island: Criminals choose beach clean over prison - BBC News Yobs forced to clean up seaside town beaches in community payback drive ordered by ministers | The Sun

Damian appears before the Justice Select Committee

Tuesday, 19 September, 2023
This week Damian appeared before the Justice Select Committee to take questions on community sentencing.  Parliamentlive.tv - Justice and Home Affairs Committee      

More industry placements for T Levels

Monday, 24 October, 2022
In Education Questions Damian commended Alton College's work with local employers and asked how more firms could be introduced to the benefits - for them, for students, for the economy - of T Levels industry placements.

Protecting children online

Thursday, 20 October, 2022
During a DCMS debate on child protection online, Damian highlights research from Ofcom that indicates the risk of children appearing to be over 18 when they register with false dates of birth.
